# LoreKit

> Shared, persistent memory for AI coding agents. Agents learn lessons across sessions, machines, and CI runs — stored in a central Supabase database and served over the MCP protocol.

## What it does

LoreKit gives any MCP-compatible agent a `memory.write / memory.read / memory.list / memory.delete / memory.search` interface backed by Supabase Postgres. Lessons are scoped (global → project → repo → branch) so an agent working on a feature branch inherits repo- and global-level context without polluting it.

## Quick start (5 minutes)

1. Clone and install: `git clone https://github.com/mthines/lorekit && cd lorekit && pnpm install`
2. Deploy to your own Supabase project: `supabase link --project-ref <ref> && pnpm nx deploy supabase`
3. Generate a token in the web dashboard: Overview → Step 2 → Generate new token
4. Add the MCP config to your agent — see the Installation Guide below

Full step-by-step: https://github.com/mthines/lorekit/blob/main/docs/deployment.md

## Installation Guide (for agents)

See: https://github.com/mthines/lorekit/blob/main/docs/install.md

This file is structured so an AI agent can execute every step autonomously or paste the config snippet and finish in seconds.

## MCP endpoint

```
https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/functions/v1/mcp
```

Auth: `Authorization: Bearer lk_rw_<token>` (read+write) or `lk_ro_<token>` (read-only)

## MCP tools

| Tool | Token required | Description |
|------|---------------|-------------|
| `memory.write` | `lk_rw_` | Store or update a lesson |
| `memory.read` | either | Read one lesson by scope + key |
| `memory.list` | either | List lessons for a scope (tag-filterable) |
| `memory.delete` | `lk_rw_` | Delete a lesson |
| `memory.search` | either | Full-text search; supports `repo::owner/*` wildcard |

## Scope format

```
global                                   # universal lessons
project::{name}                          # monorepo-level
repo::{owner}/{repo}                     # repository-level
branch::{owner}/{repo}::{branch}         # branch-level (short-lived)
```

`::` is the only valid separator. Segments are lowercased on ingest.

## Token formats

```
lk_rw_<32 chars>   # read + write
lk_ro_<32 chars>   # read only
```

Tokens never expire and are stored as SHA-256 hashes. Generate and revoke in the dashboard.

## Architecture

- **MCP server** — Supabase Edge Function (Deno), endpoint `/functions/v1/mcp`
- **Web dashboard** — Next.js 15 on Vercel (`lorekit-io.vercel.app`)
- **Database** — Supabase Postgres, row-level security, full-text search

## Key documentation

- MCP tools reference: https://github.com/mthines/lorekit/blob/main/docs/mcp-tools.md
- Scope format: https://github.com/mthines/lorekit/blob/main/docs/scope-format.md
- API tokens: https://github.com/mthines/lorekit/blob/main/docs/api-tokens.md
- Deployment: https://github.com/mthines/lorekit/blob/main/docs/deployment.md
- Architecture: https://github.com/mthines/lorekit/blob/main/docs/architecture.md
- Observability setup: https://github.com/mthines/lorekit/blob/main/docs/otel.md
- Installation guide: https://github.com/mthines/lorekit/blob/main/docs/install.md

## Error codes

| JSON-RPC code | Meaning |
|---------------|---------|
| `-32001` | Unauthorized (missing/invalid token, or read-only token on a write) |
| `-32603` | Execution error (DB error, scope validation failure) |
| `-32700` | Malformed JSON |
| `-32601` | Unknown tool name |
